title: Supabase RLS blocking inserts
symptom: INSERT operation fails with error code 42501 (insufficient_privilege). The table exists and the query syntax is correct, but RLS policies prevent the insert.
applies_when: RLS is enabled on the target table
Using anon or authenticated role (not service_role)
root_cause: Supabase enables Row Level Security (RLS) by default on new tables. Without an explicit INSERT policy, all inserts are denied for non-service-role connections. The anon and authenticated roles need explicit RLS policies to allow inserts.
fix_steps: Check current RLS policies: SELECT * FROM pg_policies WHERE tablename = 'your_table'
Add an INSERT policy: CREATE POLICY "Allow inserts" ON your_table FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (true) -- or a more restrictive check
For authenticated users only: WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = user_id)
Verify with: INSERT INTO your_table (...) VALUES (...) -- as the appropriate role
agent_instruction: Add an RLS INSERT policy to the table. For authenticated users: CREATE POLICY "allow_insert" ON table_name FOR INSERT TO authenticated WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = user_id). For public inserts, use WITH CHECK (true) but consider security implications.
safety_notes: Never use service_role key in client-side code
WITH CHECK (true) allows anyone to insert — only use for truly public tables
Use auth.jwt()->'user_metadata'->>'role' for admin checks, never subquery the profiles table (causes 42P17 recursion)
